1 # Proposals for issue 15193 2 3 ## Proposal 0 4 5 ## Proposal 6 7 ### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue. 8 Bold text appears temporarily, then reverts to normal text when sending `# ```to send``` ` message 9 10 ### What is the root cause of the problem? 11 When the message is synched with the backend it's being changed from the original one: 12 - original message: ` # bold code ` 13 - The message gets HTML-escaped before synching with the backend: ` 

bold code
 `
14	- Then message is send to the backend, and after being processed on the backend it looks as following: ` 
bold code
 `
15	
16	Therefore the text flickering (from bold to regular) happens due to this change on the backend.
17	This can be clearly seen in offline mode: the message stays bold forever, until the app goes back online
18	
19	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
20	To make sure everything is consistent I suggest to simply get rid of Bold property in the message before it's even rendered for the first time.
21	The change can be made [in this method](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/pages/home/report/ReportActionCompose.js#L471)
22	The advantage of this solution is the fact that it's going to be very clean, we won't need to make changes on the backend, and we'll maintain consistency w/o introducing any regressions...
23	
24	---
25	
26	## Proposal 1
27	
28	## Proposal
29	
30	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
31	Message turns bold temporarily when enclosed with a `#` followed by ``` or `.
32	
33	### What is the root cause of that problem?
34	The frontend parses the expression in a different way then the backend. Frontend encloses the `
` tag inside the `h1` tag while the backend does not enclose the `pre` tag inside `h1`.
35	
36	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
37	
38	We can change the ExpensiMark `#` parsing rule such that it ignores adding `h1` if `pre` or `code` tag is present.
39	The rule exists here:
40	https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L166
41	
42	The new regex would look like the following:
43	```
44	/^#(?!#) +(?!<(pre|code)>)(.+)$\s*/gm
45	```
46	
47	The [replacement part](https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L167) of this regex needs to be changed as well:
48	
49	```
50	replacement: '
$2
',
51	```
52	
53	This works well after the fix.
54	
55	https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30054992/219319194-71f8e132-3e57-4cc9-8d5e-91fd9323352f.mov
56	
57	---
58	
59	## Proposal 2
60	
61	## Proposal (from comment #1432985056 by moezh)
62	
63	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
64	Bold text appears temporarily, then reverts to normal text, when sending ``# ```code``` ``.
65	
66	### What is the root cause of that problem?
67	The problem is related to the way the frontend and backend parse expressions, resulting in differences between the two. Specifically, the frontend sends `
code
`, while the backend replies with `
code
`.
68	
69	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
70	To address the root cause of the problem, I recommend making the following changes:
71	
72	- Adjust the CSS for the "pre" class to prevent it from inheriting font weight and size from its parent.
73	- Change the CSS for the "h1" class to use lineHeight instead of marginBottom, to take into consideration that the title might be empty.
74	
75	I believe that the changes outlined above are the most effective and simple way to address the problem.
76	
77	https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2527288/219359884-b7ea7b53-0c85-4e17-8d56-9f55c2e6a86a.mov
78	
79	### What alternative solution…(+6179)
